_meta.purpose: Career atom JSON schema — exact field definitions for ingestAtom calls.
_meta.update_instructions: Edit this file to change atom field definitions. Also update openapi_schema.yaml CareerAtom component to match.

action_verb.description: Strong past-tense verb. Choose from the verbs list below.

action_detail.description: What was done — specific, concrete, no filler words. This field drives conflict detection.
action_detail.good_example: CI/CD pipeline build time by switching to parallel GitHub Actions jobs

company.description: Full company name as the user says it.

role.description: Job title at the time of this achievement.

context.description: Background / situation that makes the achievement meaningful. The S in STAR.

stakes.description: Why this mattered — business impact, risk, urgency.

you_specifically.description: User's personal contribution when the work was a team effort.

result_text.description: The outcome — ideally quantified. The R in STAR.
result_text.good_example: Deploy time dropped from 18 min to 11 min; team shipped 2x faster

tools_used.description: Technologies, frameworks, tools. Used for resume keyword matching.

timeframe.description: When this happened. Prefer ISO date (YYYY-MM) or quarter (Q3 2023).

difficulty.description: Subjective difficulty. 'hard' maps to P1 importance in resume ranking.

challenge.description: What made this hard — obstacle, constraint, or ambiguity overcome.

your_decision.description: Key decision or judgment call the user personally made.

team_role.description: User's role in the team: solo=alone, lead=directed others, contributor=part of team.

what_went_wrong.description: Optional — failure or setback, used for behavioral interview prep.

behavioral_tags.description: Behavioral competencies demonstrated. See taxonomy below.

skills_demonstrated.description: Hard and soft skills used. Critical for JD matching in resume generation.

metrics.description: Structured numeric results. Each metric is indexed separately for retrieval.

Metric.timeframe: string — optional, same format as atom timeframe
